A test function that conditionally asserts false, but the condition is never met.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that defines and executes a function named [`test`](<#05-002test>). The [`test`](<#05-002test>) function contains a conditional statement that checks if the condition `false` is true. Since the condition is always false, the `assert(false);` statement inside the block will never execute. The script demonstrates a basic structure for a test function, but it does not perform any actual testing or assertions due to the hardcoded false condition.
